verdict_reasoning: TMED10 is a type I single-pass transmembrane protein with documented localization to the plasma membrane. The luminal GOLD domain and coiled-coil region mediate its surface targeting, partially overriding ER retention. The protein constitutively cycles through the secretory pathway (ER, ERGIC, Golgi) and the PM as part of its cargo-receptor function. It reaches the outer leaflet via its own TM mechanism, not recruitment by another surface protein. Cell-type-specific surface presence documented in pancreatic β-cells. Dual localization with a documented PM pool is confirmed.
